solution:
def minChanges(nums, k):
    n = len(nums)
    total = sum(nums[:k]) ^ nums[k:]
    return n - (total & (2**n - 1))

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
We calculate the XOR of the first k elements and the last k elements, then find the remainder when the total XOR is divided by 2^n - 1. The solution has a high efficiency because it only requires O(n) time and space complexity. The energy implications are low since no additional energy consumption is needed beyond the initial input.
